changes: Tightened diff-size accounting around binary-file boundaries

files.description: Adjusted chunk-byte budget calculation; introduced a new helper for binary detection.
Added unit tests for the binary-detection helper.

findings.body: The new helper detects binary files by reading the first 8 KB and checking for a NUL byte. This works for most real-world binaries but misses UTF-16 text and produces false positives on minified JS containing raw NUL escapes. Consider relying on `git diff --numstat` (which already classifies binary files) instead of reimplementing the heuristic.
findings.reasoning: The intent is to skip binary files from the chunk budget. The current implementation's NUL-byte check has known failure modes. git itself maintains a binary classification we could read from --numstat output without reimplementing it. The recommendation is sound but the existing implementation is not demonstrably broken for the inputs the action sees.
findings.title: Binary detection heuristic may misclassify minified JS

findings.body: When `MAX_CHUNK_BYTES` is unset and the env var fallback returns `""`, the parsed value is `0`, not the documented default. The downstream comparison `chunkSize > 0` then short-circuits and emits a single mega-chunk. Validate with `Number.isFinite()` and clamp.
findings.reasoning: This is a NaN-propagation pattern flagged in defaults/review-reference.md. The default-fallback codepath is not exercised by the existing tests. The recommendation is valid but I have not traced every caller of the chunker to confirm whether MAX_CHUNK_BYTES is always set upstream.
findings.title: MAX_CHUNK_BYTES empty-string fallback bypasses default

summary: The PR refactors chunk allocation and adds binary-file detection. The behavior is plausible but two findings raise correctness concerns I could not fully trace from the diff alone.
